themeWords.meaning: Israel; he strives with God; the nation descended from Jacob
The covenant name of the God of Israel

sentenceTheme: After the judges Tola and Jair, Israel falls into idolatry, is oppressed by Philistines and Ammonites, cries to the LORD, repents, and prepares for conflict.

verses.text: And after Abimelech there arose to defend Israel Tola the son of Puah, the son of Dodo, a man of Issachar; and he dwelt in Shamir in mount Ephraim.

highlightedWords.meaning: Israel; he strives with God; the nation descended from Jacob
Ephraim; a son of Joseph; also the tribe and its territory

verses.text: And he judged Israel twenty and three years, and died, and was buried in Shamir.

highlightedWords.meaning: To judge, govern, vindicate, or rule
Israel; he strives with God; the nation descended from Jacob

verses.text: And after him arose Jair, a Gileadite, and judged Israel twenty and two years.

highlightedWords.meaning: Jair; he enlightens; a Gileadite judge of Israel
Gileadite; an inhabitant or native of Gilead

verses.text: And he had thirty sons that rode on thirty ass colts, and they had thirty cities, which are called Havoth-jair unto this day, which are in the land of Gilead.

highlightedWords.meaning: Son; child; descendant; member of a group
Gilead; a region east of the Jordan; also a personal name

verses.text: And Jair died, and was buried in Camon.

highlightedWords.meaning: Jair; he enlightens; a Gileadite judge of Israel
To die; to perish; to be dead

verses.text: And the children of Israel did evil again in the sight of the LORD, and served Baalim, and Ashtaroth, and the gods of Syria, and the gods of Zidon, and the gods of Moab, and the gods of the children of Ammon, and the gods of the Philistines, and forsook the LORD, and served not him.

highlightedWords.meaning: Israel; he strives with God; the nation descended from Jacob
The covenant name of the God of Israel

verses.text: And the anger of the LORD was hot against Israel, and he sold them into the hands of the Philistines, and into the hands of the children of Ammon.

highlightedWords.meaning: The covenant name of the God of Israel
Ammon; descendants of Lot; a people east of the Jordan

verses.text: And that year they vexed and oppressed the children of Israel: eighteen years, all the children of Israel that were on the other side Jordan in the land of the Amorites, which is in Gilead.

highlightedWords.meaning: Israel; he strives with God; the nation descended from Jacob
Gilead; a region east of the Jordan; also a personal name

verses.text: Moreover the children of Ammon passed over Jordan to fight also against Judah, and against Benjamin, and against the house of Ephraim; so that Israel was sore distressed.

highlightedWords.meaning: Ammon; descendants of Lot; a people east of the Jordan
Judah; son of Jacob; the southern tribe and kingdom

verses.text: And the children of Israel cried unto the LORD, saying, We have sinned against thee, both because we have forsaken our God, and also served Baalim.

highlightedWords.meaning: Israel; he strives with God; the nation descended from Jacob
The covenant name of the God of Israel

verses.text: And the LORD said unto the children of Israel, Did not I deliver you from the Egyptians, and from the Amorites, from the children of Ammon, and from the Philistines?

highlightedWords.meaning: The covenant name of the God of Israel
Egypt; the people or land of Egypt

verses.text: The Zidonians also, and the Amalekites, and the Maonites, did oppress you; and ye cried to me, and I delivered you out of their hand.

highlightedWords.meaning: To snatch away, deliver, or rescue
Hand; power; strength; control

verses.text: Yet ye have forsaken me, and served other gods: wherefore I will deliver you no more.

highlightedWords.meaning: To leave, forsake, abandon, or neglect
God; gods; judges; divine beings (context determines singular/plural)

verses.text: Go and cry unto the gods which ye have chosen; let them deliver you in the time of your tribulation.

highlightedWords.meaning: God; gods; judges; divine beings (context determines singular/plural)
Distress, trouble, affliction

verses.text: And the children of Israel said unto the LORD, We have sinned: do thou unto us whatsoever seemeth good unto thee; deliver us only, we pray thee, this day.

highlightedWords.meaning: Israel; he strives with God; the nation descended from Jacob
The covenant name of the God of Israel

verses.text: And they put away the strange gods from among them, and served the LORD: and his soul was grieved for the misery of Israel.

highlightedWords.meaning: The covenant name of the God of Israel
Soul; life; self; person; inner being

verses.text: Then the children of Ammon were gathered together, and encamped in Gilead. And the children of Israel assembled themselves together, and encamped in Mizpeh.

highlightedWords.meaning: Ammon; descendants of Lot; a people east of the Jordan
Gilead; a region east of the Jordan; also a personal name

verses.text: And the people and princes of Gilead said one to another, What man is he that will begin to fight against the children of Ammon? he shall be head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.

highlightedWords.meaning: Gilead; a region east of the Jordan; also a personal name
Head; chief; leader; top; summit
